seoTitle: Add Bank Accounts in QuickBooks Online
metaDescription: How to create a new bank or credit card account in QuickBooks Online's Chart of Accounts, either as a manual register or live bank feed.
intro: Setting up a new banking line in QuickBooks Online can go one of two ways: a manual register you update yourself, or a live connection that pulls transactions automatically. This guide covers creating the account in your Chart of Accounts and choosing between those two approaches.

steps.heading: Open the Chart of Accounts
steps.body: From the left sidebar, click Accounting, then Chart of Accounts.

steps.heading: Start a new account
steps.body: Click the green New button in the upper right of the ledger dashboard.

steps.heading: Choose the account type
steps.body: Select Banks or Credit Cards as the primary account type, then pick the exact detail type and name your account.

steps.heading: Set up a manual register
steps.body: For a manual account, enter an opening balance and starting date, then click Save.

steps.heading: Or connect a live feed
steps.body: Go to All Apps, then Bank Transactions, click Link Account, search for your bank, complete the secure sign-in, and choose which accounts to sync along with a historical data window.

faq.question: Should I create a manual account or connect a live feed?
faq.answer: A live feed automates transaction downloads, while a manual register is useful if your bank isn't supported or you prefer entering transactions yourself.

faq.question: What's an opening balance and why does it matter?
faq.answer: It's the starting balance for the account as of your chosen start date, which keeps your trial statements balanced from day one.

faq.question: How much historical data can I pull when connecting live?
faq.answer: You choose a historical data download window during setup, controlling how many past months of records QuickBooks pulls in.

faq.question: Can I switch a manual account to a live connection later?
faq.answer: Yes, you can go to Bank Transactions afterward and link the same account to a live feed if your bank becomes available.
